rows.mechanism: BER substrate accumulation in MBD4-LOF (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine / cytarabine triphosphate incorporation into BER repair patches stalls replication. MBD4-specific: rescue by WT-MBD4 but not catalytic-dead mutant.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: MBD4 LOF causes BER substrate accumulation (dU:dG mispairs). Gemcitabine and cytarabine exploit this: dFdCTP/araC incorporation into dU-rich repair patches stalls replication. Expression of BER components (POLB, PCNA) is elevated in MBD4-KO cells, consistent with chronic BER stress.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

in_vitro.summary: MBD4-KO isogenic cell lines show ~5× increased sensitivity to gemcitabine and cytarabine vs WT in rescue experiments. Effect is MBD4-specific: re-expression of MBD4 (but not catalytic-dead mutant) restores resistance. Two independent isogenic systems validated.

in_vivo.summary: MBD4-deficient uveal melanoma PDX derived from the index patient showed dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine monotherapy. PDX response mirrors patient response; tumor regression observed. WT-MBD4 PDX controls did not respond.

clinical.summary: Index patient (uveal melanoma, MBD4 germline LOF): dramatic, durable response to gemcitabine — exceptional for uveal melanoma, which is normally chemo-refractory. Response mirrors PDX and isogenic model data. Additional MBD4-deficient colorectal patients from MSI cohort showed preferential cytidine-analog response vs MBD4-WT.

Branch: strong_clinical and n_positive≥3.
rows.mechanistic_hypothesis_note: Differentiation: `mechanism` and narrative `summary` fields describe biology or literature; they do not imply ModalityStatus.POSITIVE unless the corresponding modality cell is `positive`.
rows.interpretation: Validated across multiple modalities (expression, in_vitro, in_vivo, clinical). No CRISPR or pharmacologic screen data — validation rests on clinical, in vivo, and in vitro evidence.

rows.mechanism: Pan-cancer screens and expression–sensitivity correlates link PARP pathway activity or PARP1 levels to drug response in HRD/DDR contexts; those signals are not MBD4-specific. Separately, some preclinical work discusses BER load or RNF144A–PARP1 routing as a hypothetical sensitization narrative in MBD4-deficient models — a distinct layer, not interchangeable with pan-tumor PARP1 biomarker claims.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: Large-scale and pan-tumor analyses associate PARP1 expression or PARP inhibitor sensitivity with DDR/HRD-related contexts; those patterns are not defined as MBD4-specific biomarkers. For MBD4-deficient tumors, literature discusses BER stress and (in some models) PARP1 or RNF144A–PARP1 regulation as a separate, context-dependent hypothesis — orthogonal to generic PARP1-as-pan-cancer-predictor framing.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

in_vitro.summary: No robust MBD4 isogenic KO/WT synthetic lethality with PARP inhibitors is established in the reviewed literature. RNF144A perturbation models (e.g. BRCA-WT backgrounds) report olaparib sensitization via distinct circuitry; those findings are not receipts for MBD4-isogenic PARP SL.

in_vivo.summary: No MBD4-deficient PDX data for PARP inhibitor response.

clinical.summary: No published patient-level response data for PARP inhibitors specifically in MBD4-deficient tumors. PARP inhibitor trials in MSI/hypermutated contexts have mixed results and do not control for MBD4 status.

rows.falsification_narrative: Falsified mechanism — PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, talazoparib) are NOT recommended for MBD4-LOF tumors on a transcriptional-upregulation basis. The competing hypothesis — that MBD4-LOF benefits from PARP inhibitors via PARP1 transcriptional upregulation (i.e., a PARP-trapping mechanism) — is falsified directly by the manuscript data: PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF cell lines is NOT elevated relative to comparator (n=19 vs 1498, p=0.605). The recommended axis is ATR/WEE1 inhibition (lead: ceralasertib); see the ATR/WEE1 row of this matrix for the full evidence chain.
rows.tier_fusion_rule_detail: n_positive_modalities=0 (only ModalityStatus.POSITIVE counts; MISSING/MIXED/NEGATIVE do not). weighted_modality_score=1.20. crispr=missing, prism=missing, gdsc=missing, expression=mixed, in_vitro=mixed, clinical=missing, in_vivo=missing. Applied rule_id=base_fallback_mechanistic_candidate_else_branch → base_tier=Mechanistic candidate only.
rows.mechanistic_hypothesis_note: Differentiation: `mechanism` and narrative `summary` fields describe biology or literature; they do not imply ModalityStatus.POSITIVE unless the corresponding modality cell is `positive`.
rows.interpretation: PARP Inhibitors (olaparib, niraparib, talazoparib, rucaparib): Mechanistic candidate only (manuscript-falsified). Falsified mechanism — PARP inhibitors (e.g., olaparib, talazoparib) are NOT recommended for MBD4-LOF tumors on a transcriptional-upregulation basis. The competing hypothesis — that MBD4-LOF benefits from PARP inhibitors via PARP1 transcriptional upregulation (i.e., a PARP-trapping mechanism) — is falsified directly by the manuscript data: PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF cell lines is NOT elevated relative to comparator (n=19 vs 1498, p=0.605). The recommended axis is ATR/WEE1 inhibition (lead: ceralasertib); see the ATR/WEE1 row of this matrix for the full evidence chain.

rows.mechanism: BER stress → ssDNA accumulation at stalled forks → ATR kinase activation. ATR/CHK1 and WEE1/CDK1 checkpoints are required to resolve replication stress. MBD4-LOF BER load may create constitutive low-level replication stress, sensitizing to checkpoint abrogation especially in TP53-loss context.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC2 pharmacologic stratification, drug=ceralasertib (ATR inhibitor): MBD4 True-LOF cell lines (n=14) show increased sensitivity vs MBD4-WT (n=914) on the Methods-rule cohort (no somatic MBD4). LN_IC50: Δ=-0.732, p=0.0215, Cohen's d=-0.503. AUC and Z_SCORE are direction-consistent: AUC Δ=-0.0556 p=0.01294 d=-0.557; Z_SCORE Δ=-0.501 p=0.0215. Manuscript-derived quantitative evidence supporting BER stress → ATR checkpoint dependency. See auxiliary_evidence for stress tests, WEE1 axis-partner, and PARP1 falsification arm.

in_vitro.summary: Mechanistically plausible: chronic BER stress in MBD4-LOF → elevated ssDNA at stalled forks → ATR activation. No published MBD4-specific isogenic ATR/WEE1 inhibitor data.

clinical.summary: No MBD4-specific clinical ATR/WEE1 inhibitor data.

auxiliary_evidence.summary: Stress test #1 — MSI purge. Re-runs ceralasertib LN_IC50 with all MSI-H lines excluded from both arms (rationale: MBD4-LOF correlates with MSI-H; signal could be confounded by global MSI sensitivity to ATR). Result: signal STRENGTHENS without MSI-H lines (Δ=-0.910 vs primary -0.732; d=-0.623 vs primary -0.503). n_LOF=10, n_WT=906, p=0.0153. Rules out MSI-H as a confounder.

Stress test #2 — TP53 stratification. Restricts both arms to TP53-mutant lines (rationale: TP53 status modifies ATR-pathway dependency; >95% of HGSOC is TP53-mutant). Result: effect size LARGEST in TP53-mutant subpopulation. LN_IC50: Δ=-1.069, p=0.0030, Cohen's d=-0.740. AUC: Δ=-0.0869, p=0.000873, d=-0.889. n_LOF=11, n_WT=619. Especially relevant for HGSOC clinical translation.

Stress test #3 — Leave-one-out. Removes one MBD4-LOF line at a time and re-runs the primary endpoint (rationale: a single highly-sensitive line could drive the n=14 result). Result: 14/14 iterations remain significant. max p=0.0452, min p=0.0082, all p<0.05. No single cell line is driving the effect. n_LOF=13 per iteration, n_WT=914.

Stress test #4 — Lineage stratification (non-bowel). Splits the primary endpoint by bowel/non-bowel lineage (rationale: MSI-H bowel lines are over-represented in MBD4-LOF pool; effect could be bowel-specific artifact). Result: non-bowel cohort holds — n_LOF=9, n_WT=873, Δ=-0.871, p=0.0253, Cohen's d=-0.599. Effect generalizes outside bowel lineages. (The bowel subset n_LOF=5 is underpowered, p=0.126; see audit.)

Axis partner — adavosertib (MK-1775; WEE1 inhibitor), same replication-stress checkpoint axis as ATR. Direction-consistent with the ATR primary endpoint (negative Δ = more sensitive in MBD4-LOF) and similar effect magnitude (Cohen's d=-0.359), but p=0.074 sub-threshold. n_LOF=15, n_WT=920, Δ=-0.508. Status=MIXED encodes the manuscript's honest framing: this is a supporting trend, not an independent confirmation.

Falsification arm — PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF lines. Tests the competing hypothesis that MBD4-LOF sensitivity is driven by PARP1 upregulation (PARP-trapping pathway). Result: PARP1 is NOT elevated in MBD4-LOF — Mann-Whitney U p=0.605, median LOF 6.770 vs comparator 6.656. n_LOF=19, n_comparator=1498. Encoded POSITIVE because this *rules out* PARP-trapping as the mechanism, which IS positive evidence for ATR over PARP (justifies recommending ATR inhibitors rather than olaparib/talazoparib). Sanity check: pan-cancer PARP1×PARPi correlation ρ=-0.416, p=1.4e-21 (n=481) confirms the framework — high PARP1 → more PARPi-sensitive is real, MBD4-LOF lines just don't show it.

rows.tier_fusion_rule_detail: n_positive_modalities=6 (only ModalityStatus.POSITIVE counts; MISSING/MIXED/NEGATIVE do not). weighted_modality_score=7.40. crispr=missing, prism=missing, gdsc=positive, expression=missing, in_vitro=missing, clinical=missing, in_vivo=missing. Branch: (crispr_pos or pharma_pos) and n_positive≥2 and not crispr_neg.
rows.mechanistic_hypothesis_note: Differentiation: `mechanism` and narrative `summary` fields describe biology or literature; they do not imply ModalityStatus.POSITIVE unless the corresponding modality cell is `positive`. `in_vitro.summary` may read as mechanistically plausible while status is `missing` — it does not increment n_positive_modalities.
rows.interpretation:
ATR / WEE1 Inhibitors (berzosertib, adavosertib, ceralasertib): Strong candidate dependency axis (manuscript-confirmed primary new candidate axis).
Primary endpoint (GDSC2, LN_IC50): n=14/914, Δln_IC50=-0.732, ΔAUC=-0.056, p=0.0215, d=-0.503.
Stress tests (4/4 positive):
  - Stress test #1 — MSI purge: n=10/906, Δln_IC50=-0.910, p=0.015, d=-0.623
  - Stress test #2 — TP53 stratification: n=11/619, Δln_IC50=-1.069, p=0.003, d=-0.740
  - Stress test #3 — Leave-one-out: n=13/914, p=0.045
  - Stress test #4 — Lineage stratification (non-bowel): n=9/873, Δln_IC50=-0.871, p=0.025, d=-0.599
Axis-partner confirmation:
  - Axis partner — adavosertib (MK-1775; WEE1 inhibitor), same replication-stress checkpoint axis as ATR: n=15/920, Δln_IC50=-0.508, p=0.074, d=-0.359 [status=mixed]
Falsification arm — why we are NOT recommending PARP inhibitors on a transcriptional-upregulation basis:
  - Falsification arm — PARP1 expression in MBD4-LOF lines: n=19/1498, p=0.605
Recommendation: prioritize the ATR/WEE1 axis (ceralasertib lead, adavosertib partner); PARP inhibitor selection cannot rest on MBD4-LOF-driven PARP1 transcriptional upregulation because that mechanism is falsified by the data above.

rows.mechanism: WRN SL is established for MSI-H context (reversed replication forks, aberrant AT-rich secondary structures). MBD4 LOF → hypermutator → subset of tumors become MSI-H. WRN SL with MBD4-direct LOF (MSI-independent) not yet validated. Signal may be MSI-H driven, not MBD4-specific.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

in_vitro.summary: WRN SL is established for MSI-H context (Chan et al. Nature 2019). MBD4 LOF → hypermutator phenotype → subset of tumors become MSI-H. WRN SL with MBD4-direct LOF (MSI-independent) not established in vitro.
in_vitro.notes: Signal is MSI-H specific; MBD4 LOF alone insufficient.

clinical.summary: No clinical WRN inhibitor data for MBD4-deficient tumors.

rows.mechanism: MBD4 LOF → CpG→TpG hypermutator clock → highest TMB in many tumor types. High TMB → increased neoantigen burden → IO response. Pan-cancer TMB-high FDA approval supports pembrolizumab use. Not a direct SL axis; relies on hypermutation → neoantigens mechanism.

crispr.summary: CRISPR analysis not run or no data available.

expression.summary: MBD4 LOF → CpG→TpG hypermutator clock → highest TMB in many tumor types. High TMB is associated with IO response (pan-cancer FDA approval). TMB > 10 mut/Mb threshold frequently exceeded in MBD4-deficient tumors.

prism.summary: PRISM data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

gdsc.summary: GDSC data not loaded (data not available in this deployment).

clinical.summary: Exceptional IO responses reported in MBD4-germline-LOF uveal melanoma and colorectal cancer cases. Pan-cancer TMB-high FDA approval supports IO use. Not a direct SL axis; relies on hypermutator → TMB mechanism. Small n — case reports, not controlled trial data.

rows.mechanism: PKMYT1 kinase inhibition (RP-6306 class); replication stress axis. CCNE1-amplified tumors depend on PKMYT1 for S-phase checkpoint regulation. PKMYT1 inhibition causes premature mitotic entry and replication catastrophe in replication-stressed cells.

surfacing_policy: All 6 rows are surfaced. Modalities with status='missing' (e.g. CRISPR for atr_wee1, PRISM for most axes) are shown as empty cells labeled 'missing' — not hidden. The parp_inhibitors row is not tucked into a 'quarantine' tab; it is a first-class row carrying the falsification result.
